debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
l : List (List α)
l1 : List α
idx idx1 : ℕ
hidx : idx < l.length
hidx1 : idx1 < l1.length
⊢ ∃ idx',
    (l.set idx (l1.eraseIdx idx1)).flatten = (l.set idx l1).flatten.eraseIdx ↑idx' ∧
      l1[idx1] = (l.set idx l1).flatten[idx']

failed_attempts:
simp [Nat.sub_eq_zero_iff_le]
    <;> omega

  have h₂ : (l.set idx l1).flatten.length = l.length + l1.length - (if r : (l.get ⟨idx, by omega⟩).length = 0 then 1 else 0) := by
    have h₃ : (l.set idx l1).length = l.length := by simp
    have h₄ : ∀ (l : List (List α)) (a : List α), (l.set ⟨0, by omega⟩ a).Ok := by
      intro l a
      simp
      <;>
      (try decide) <;>
      (try cases l) <;>
      (try simp_all [List.length]) <;>
      (try omega)
    -- Use the above lemmas to calculate the length of the flattened list
    calc
      (l.set idx l1).flatten.length = ((l.set idx l1).toList).flatten.length := by rfl
      _ = (l.set idx l1).toList.length + l1.length - (if r : (l.get ⟨idx, by omega⟩).length = 0 then 1 else 0) := by
        -- Calculate the length of the flattened list
        have h₅ : (l.set idx l1).toList = List.map (fun x => x) l := by simp
        rw [h₅]
        have h₆ : (List.map (fun x => x) l).flatten.length = l.length + l1.length - (if r : (l.get ⟨idx, by omega⟩).length = 0 then 1 else 0) := by
          -- Use the fact that the length of the flattened list is the sum of the lengths of the sublists
          have h₇ : (List.map (fun x => x) l).flatten.length = l.length + l1.length - (if r : (l.get ⟨idx, by omega⟩).length = 0 then 1 else 0) := by
            -- Use the fact that the length of the flattened list is the sum of the lengths of the sublists
            simp_all [List.map, List.get]
            <;>
            (try cases l) <;>
            (try simp_all [List.length, List.map, List.get]) <;>
            (try omega)
          exact h₇
        rw [h₆]
      _ = (l.set idx l1).toList.length + l1.length - (if r : (l.get ⟨idx, by omega⟩).length = 0 then 1 else 0) := by rfl

  -- Find the index of the element to be erased
  have h₃ : ∃ (i : ℕ), i < (l.set idx l1).flatten.length ∧ i < (l.set idx l1).flatten.length := by
    use 0
    <;> simp [h₂]
    <;>
    (try omega)
    <;>
    (try {
      cases l with
      | nil => contradiction
      | cons hd tl =>
        simp_all [List.get]
        <;>
        (try omega)
        <;>
        (try {
          cases hd with
          | nil => simp_all [List.get]
          | cons hd' tl' => simp_all [List.get]
        })
    })

  -- Use the above lemmas to prove the main theorem
  obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := h₃
  refine' ⟨⟨i, by omega⟩, _, _⟩ <;>
    (try simp_all) <;>
    (try {
      cases l with
      | nil => contradiction
      | cons hd tl =>
        simp_all [List.get]
        <;>
        (try omega)
        <;>
        (try {
          cases hd with
          | nil => simp_all [List.get]
          | cons hd' tl' => simp_all [List.get]
        })
    }) <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [List.get]
      <;>
      (try omega)
    }) <;>
    (try {
      cases l1 with
      | nil => contradiction
      | cons hd tl =>
        simp_all [List.get]
        <;>
        (try omega)
    }) <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [List.get]
      <;>
      (try omega)
    }) <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [List.get]
      <;>
      (try omega)
    }) <;>
    (try {
      cases l with
      | nil => contradiction
      | cons hd tl =>
        simp_all [List.get]
        <;>
        (try omega)
        <;>
        (try {
          cases hd with
          | nil => simp_all [List.get]
          | cons hd' tl' => simp_all [List.get]
        })
    }) <;>
    (try {
      simp_all [List.get]
      <;>
      (try omega)
    })

simpa using hidx1, by simp [hidx1]⟩
    <;> simp [hidx1]
  have h₂ : ∃ (j : Fin ((l.set idx l1).flatten.length)), l1[idx1] = (l.set idx l1).flatten[j] := by
    have h₃ : (l1[idx1] : α) ∈ (l.set idx l1).flatten := by
      have h₄ : (l1[idx1] : α) ∈ l1 := by
        simp [hidx1]
      have h₅ : (l1 : List α) ∈ (l.set idx l1) := by
        simp_all [List.set_mem]
        <;> aesop
      have h₆ : (l1[idx1] : α) ∈ (l.set idx l1).flatten := by
        have h₇ : (l1[idx1] : α) ∈ l1 := h₄
        have h₈ : l1 ∈ (l.set idx l1) := h₅
        have h₉ : (l1[idx1] : α) ∈ (l.set idx l1).flatten := by
          apply List.mem_of_mem_map (fun x => x)
          simp_all [List.mem_cons, List.mem_singleton]
          <;> aesop
        exact h₉
      exact h₆
    have h₇ : ∃ (j : Fin ((l.set idx l1).flatten.length)), (l1[idx1] : α) = (l.set idx l1).flatten[j] := by
      classical
      have h₈ : (l1[idx1] : α) ∈ (l.set idx l1).flatten := h₃
      have h₉ : ∃ (j : Fin ((l.set idx l1).flatten.length)), (l.set idx l1).flatten[j] = l1[idx1] := by
        -- Use the fact that the element is in the list to find its index
        have h₁₀ : ∃ (j : ℕ), j < (l.set idx l1).flatten.length ∧ (l.set idx l1).flatten.get ⟨j, by omega⟩ = l1[idx1] := by
          classical
          have h₁₁ : (l1[idx1] : α) ∈ (l.set idx l1).flatten := h₃
          have h₁₂ : ∃ (j : ℕ), j < (l.set idx l1).flatten.length ∧ (l.set idx l1).flatten.get ⟨j, by omega⟩ = l1[idx1] := by
            rw [List.mem_iff_get] at h₁₁
            obtain ⟨j, hj⟩ := h₁₁
            refine' ⟨j.val, by omega, _⟩
            <;> simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
            <;> aesop
          exact h₁₂
        obtain ⟨j, hj₁, hj₂⟩ := h₁₀
        refine' ⟨⟨j, hj₁⟩, _⟩
        simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
        <;> aesop
      obtain ⟨j, hj⟩ := h₉
      refine' ⟨j, _⟩
      simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]
      <;> aesop
    exact h₇
  obtain ⟨j₁, _⟩ := h₂
  refine' ⟨j₁, _, _⟩
  <;> simp_all [List.eraseIdx_eq]
  <;>
  (try decide) <;>
  (try omega) <;>
  (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff]) <;>
  (try aesop) <;>
  (try {
    cases l <;> cases i <;> simp_all [List.eraseIdx_eq, List.get?, List.length]
    <;>
    (try omega) <;>
    (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff])
    <;>
    (try aesop)
  }) <;>
  (try {
    cases l <;> simp_all [List.eraseIdx_eq, List.get?, List.length]
    <;>
    (try omega) <;>
    (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff])
    <;>
    (try aesop)
  }) <;>
  (try {
    cases l1 <;> cases i <;> simp_all [List.eraseIdx_eq, List.get?, List.length]
    <;>
    (try omega) <;>
    (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff])
    <;>
    (try aesop)
  }) <;>
  (try {
    cases l1 <;> simp_all [List.eraseIdx_eq, List.get?, List.length]
    <;>
    (try omega) <;>
    (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff])
    <;>
    (try aesop)
  })
